records.statement: An explicit --config pointing at a file that does not exist is rejected (no silent discovery fallback).
records.notes: both-reject agreement case. Backed by case-errors-bad-config-path and wvr-bad-config-path (migrated from fixtures/parity-corpus/errors/bad-config-path/expect.json).

records.statement: Reading a well-formed devcontainer.json exits 0 and emits the resolved configuration as a single JSON document.
A JSON object with a duplicate top-level key is accepted with last-wins semantics, matching the reference after pruning.
records.notes: both-accept agreement case. Backed by case-errors-duplicate-keys and wvr-duplicate-keys.

records.statement: A cyclic extends chain (a -> b -> a) is detected and rejected during read-configuration; the reference CLI v0.87.0 does not resolve extends and accepts.
records.notes: extends is the in-flight proposal devcontainers/spec#22 (unspecified in the pinned spec). deacon resolves eagerly and detects the loop; the reference echoes extends literally. Recorded by ext-extends-resolution; backed by case-errors-extends-cycle and wvr-extends-cycle (issue #297).

records.statement: read-configuration --include-merged-configuration resolves the full extends chain (base image, merged containerEnv and forwardPorts); the reference CLI v0.87.0 does not resolve extends and errors with 'No image information specified'.
records.notes: Ahead-of-spec deacon capability (devcontainers/spec#22, issue #297). Recorded by ext-extends-resolution; backed by case-tier1-extends-child and wvr-extends-child-merged (migrated from fixtures/parity-corpus/waivers/extends-child-merged.json). See docs/DIFFERENTIATORS.md.

records.statement: An extends chain pointing at a nonexistent target file is rejected during read-configuration; the reference CLI v0.87.0 does not resolve extends and accepts.
records.notes: extends is the in-flight proposal devcontainers/spec#22 (unspecified in the pinned spec). deacon resolves eagerly and errors on the missing target; the reference echoes extends literally. Recorded by ext-extends-resolution; backed by case-errors-extends-missing and wvr-extends-missing (issue #297).

records.statement: A devcontainer.json with a hard JSONC syntax error is rejected at parse rather than silently recovered; the reference CLI's recovering parser drops the broken property and accepts.
records.notes: deacon fails fast (constitution IV, no silent fallbacks); the reference is lenient at read-configuration. Backed by case-errors-malformed-json and wvr-malformed-json.

records.statement: read-configuration --include-merged-configuration over the tier1 corpus emits a merged configuration that matches the reference after normalization (excluding extends, which is the deacon-extension bhv-readconfig-extends-merged).
records.notes: Backed by case-merged-corpus (parity_corpus_merged over the tier1 corpus).

records.statement: A workspace folder with no devcontainer configuration at all is rejected rather than resolved to an invented empty config.
records.notes: both-reject agreement case. Backed by case-errors-missing-config and wvr-missing-config.

records.statement: Reading real-world devcontainer.json configurations from the tier1 corpus produces resolved configurations that match the reference after normalization.
records.notes: Backed by case-tier1-corpus (parity_corpus_tier1 over the tier1 corpus).

records.statement: Unknown / forward-compatible top-level fields are accepted and preserved verbatim in read-configuration output, matching the reference's fidelity.
records.notes: both-accept agreement case; guards against silently dropping unmodeled fields (spec extensibility model). Backed by case-errors-unknown-field-preserved and wvr-unknown-field-preserved.

records.statement: A `features` value that is a bare string instead of an object is rejected (type-strict, matching the schema shape); the reference keeps the raw JSON and accepts.
records.notes: Deliberate consistency fix: modeled object-shaped fields enforce their spec shape and fail fast on a clear mistake, matching the typed strictness forwardPorts already had. Backed by case-errors-wrong-type-features and wvr-wrong-type-features.

records.statement: A `forwardPorts` value that is a bare string instead of an array is rejected (typed deserialization); the reference keeps the raw JSON and accepts.
records.notes: Typed (deacon) vs untyped (reference) read-configuration. Backed by case-errors-wrong-type-forwardports and wvr-wrong-type-forwardports.
